canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, lm1b is consistently characterized as a dataset for evaluating language models. The most prevalent framing identifies it as the "One-Billion Word Language Model Benchmark (LM1B)" used specifically to assess `Language modeling` performance. This operationalization is common, with one paper stating its use to "evaluate on prompts from a wide range of datasets and tasks, including language modeling with one-billion language benchmark (LM1B)" (Block Verification Accelerates Speculative Decoding). A slightly different framing describes it as a dataset for validating experimental methods across different data sources, noting its use alongside other benchmarks to "observe similar results" (Selective Attention Improves Transformer). A single paper also lists lm1b as a measure for `Multilingual generalization`, although no direct evidence for this application is provided in the input.
papers: Block Diffusion: Interpolating Between Autoregressive and Diffusion Language Models
Energy-Based Diffusion Language Models for Text Generation

definitions.text: A dataset used for running experiments to observe results similar to those on C4, validating the method across different data sources.
The One-Billion Word Language Model Benchmark (LM1B), a dataset used for evaluating language modeling performance.

relationships.evidence: We also ran experiments with WikiText (Merity et al., 2016), and lm1b (Chelba et al., 2014) and observed similar results. (Section 5)

relationships.paper: Energy-Based Diffusion Language Models for Text Generation

iclr25: Energy-Based Diffusion Language Models for Text Generation
